text:
🇦🇪 UAE — TRANSACTION COSTS 
1. Why this matters
Defines total acquisition cost, distinguishes mandatory government fees from negotiable market costs, and identifies optional expenses in a property transaction.
2. Official Government Fees (Mandatory)
(Official Fact)
The UAE does not impose stamp duty on property transactions.
(Official Fact)
Property transfers are subject to emirate-level transfer fees.
Dubai Land Department (DLD):
Transfer fee of 4% of property value
(Official Fact)
Registration-related administrative fees apply, including:
Title deed issuance fees
Trustee office / registration processing fees
(Advisory Note)
These charges are mandatory for legal transfer and registration of ownership.
3. Common Market Practice Costs (Negotiable)
(Market Practice)
Broker Commission:
Typically around 2% of property value
Common in Dubai and other emirates
(Advisory Note)
Broker fees are:
Market-driven
Negotiable
Not fixed by regulation
4. Optional Professional Costs
(Market Practice / Optional)
Legal Fees:
Typically ~0.5% – 1% of property value, or fixed fee
Varies by legal service provider
(Advisory Note)
Legal representation is not mandatory
Common in higher-value or complex transactions
5. Advisory Caution
(Advisory Caution)
Transaction cost structure varies by:
Emirate
Property type
Service providers involved
(Advisory Caution)
Only government fees (e.g., transfer fee, registration charges) are mandatory, while broker and legal costs are negotiable or optional.
6. What Needs Confirmation (Transaction-Level)
Exact administrative fees applicable to specific transaction
Broker commission agreed between parties
Legal fees depending on service provider and deal complexity
7. Sources
https://dubailand.gov.ae
8. Verification Date
13/03/2026
9. Escalation Note
Not Required –
Transaction cost components are clearly defined. Variations in broker and legal fees are market-driven and do not affect regulatory structure.
